title: 英文好嘅巴絲麻煩入一入黎(外國電視台綵訪)

replies.msg:
好抱歉冇番黎同大家一齊渡過呢個艱難嘅時間。

呢家人喺烏克蘭基輔，印定左小小海報(英/俄文)痴喺市中心，希望令當地人都可以知道香港發生緊咩事。

同時亦聯絡左幾年前烏克蘭示威中拍片向世界求助嘅少女，睇下佢會唔會有啲意見提供。

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yulia_Marushevska

今日佢番左黎基輔，有幸一會，佢幫我聯絡左傳媒朋友話可以做個訪問。
電視台 1

電視台 2


好緊張驚會講得唔好(我都開始刨緊新聞)，希望咁多位巴絲可以幫手諗下啲內容。
推
推
推
推 加油

講下兩邊武力唔對等
使用過重武力(豆袋彈)
使用化武(胡椒噴霧/催淚彈)
向記者開槍 (仲搶救緊)

2. 傷亡 (Casualities, injuries
3. 警犬行動(？ (Actions taken by police against protesters

replies.msg:
1. 政府可以配合中國作政治檢控，而受害者可以係異見人士，商家（例子就係中國而家扣留加拿大公民黎做政治籌碼）

2. 長期不合作運動，更多抗爭？

3. 中國式法治強行在香港實施，令香港法治大幅倒退
未來烏克蘭人，幫推
我都搵左個烏克蘭朋友幫手
Push
網上有英文版 睇下岩唔岩洗

OP, what sort of narrative do you have in your mind?
What goal do you wanna achieve?
Do you simply want others to know about the issue?
Or do you want to impart in them a certain "impression"?
The 3 questions they proposed can be tackled in quite a number of directions.
Anyhow, you still need to know the bill inside out, or else people are gonna be suspicious of your "ulterior" motive.
There was a thread not long ago about some kid who thought he could "debate" others on that matter.
People in the west are sometimes quite naive, in the sense that they have the habit of taking things very literally. This is especially true when it comes to legal matters.
You can't blame them, they are simply used to things being crystal clear. But then the side effect is that they can't quite understand the "Chinese way" of thinking (ie. insincerity, a fairly liberal use of figure of speech, out right deception etc.).
If you're getting an interview, make sure to do it the "western way". Explain everything clearly. Don't make too many assumptions.
MAKE SURE YOU KNOW THE BILL INSIDE OUT, don't give those shills an easy way to spin things their way.
